disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Vomiting dark brown, coffee-ground-like fluid, two episodes over the morning
trigger_question_examples: What did the vomit look like?
Has he been sick, and what colour was it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He brought up dark brown fluid twice this morning — it looked like coffee grounds, all over his lunch tray.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Several black, tarry, sticky, foul-smelling stools over a few hours
trigger_question_examples: What have his bowel motions been like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His last few bowel movements were pure black, sticky and tar-like, and they smelt awful — the pad is soaked with it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound lightheadedness and near-fainting on sitting or standing (postural symptoms)
trigger_question_examples: Does he feel faint or dizzy?
What happens when he tries to sit up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He feels like he's going to pass out, and he nearly did faint when we tried to sit him up — he's had to stay flat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pallor, diaphoresis, cold peripheries and drowsiness — appearance of shock
trigger_question_examples: How does he look right now?
Is he sweaty, pale or drowsy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's gone really pale and sweaty, his hands are cold, and he's very drowsy and just wants to lie flat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chronic daily high-dose non-steroidal anti-inflammatory (naproxen 500 mg twice daily) for osteoarthritis
trigger_question_examples: What regular medications is he on?
Does he take anything for his arthritis or any anti-inflammatory tablets?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He takes naproxen 500 mg twice a day, every day, for his bad arthritis.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild diffuse upper-abdominal tenderness, no rigidity or guarding (as reported by staff)
trigger_question_examples: Is his abdomen sore or hard when pressed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His upper tummy seems a bit tender when we press gently, but it's soft — not rigid.
